name: patternguard
description: Guard AI-agent code changes against pattern drift: plan the blast radius, execute within bounds, and review sibling consistency before committing.

interface.shortDescription: Guard agent code changes against pattern drift
interface.longDescription:
Three coordinated skills for codebases operated by AI agents and subagents.

patternguard-planner — Maps every file a change touches before writing a single line. Classifies logic (route-specific, cross-cutting, pure utility, business utility), records duplicated patterns in the index, marks template candidates only when repetition justifies it, and promotes them to template mode only when a generator command already exists. Ensures .patternguard/ exists, outputs .patternguard/CODEBASE_INDEX.yaml, .patternguard/changes/index.yaml, focused change-impact files, a structured handoff note, and respects the workflow lock. Works on greenfield and existing projects.

patternguard-executor — Executes code changes with full scope awareness. It acquires the workflow lock, invokes the planner when no index exists or drift is detected, then selects Solo, Orchestrator, or Subagent execution. Candidate-only patterns stay duplicated; template-backed patterns regenerate only when both template and generator.command are present. Only the orchestrator writes to PatternGuard index files.

patternguard-reviewer — Reviews changes after execution. Verifies correctness, sibling consistency, index conformance, generated-file integrity, and PatternGuard headers. Never edits code — only reads and produces verdicts: approved, changes-needed, or ESCALATE. It escalates when retry_count reaches 3 or the issue cannot be fixed by execution alone.
defaultPrompt: Use patternguard-planner to map the structure for a new payment validation feature
Use patternguard-executor to apply the fraud threshold change across all routes
Use patternguard-reviewer to verify the executor's changes before committing
